targetSites.surroundingContext:
# ── Description extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────


def extract_skill_description(frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    """Extract a truncated description from parsed frontmatter."""
    raw_desc = frontmatter.get("description", "")
    if not raw_desc:
        return ""
    desc = str(raw_desc).strip().strip("'\"")
    if len(desc) > 60:
        return desc[:57] + "..."
    return desc


# ── File iteration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
targetSites.whyThisIsTheTruncation: AUTHORITATIVE SITE. This is the single function that performs the 60-character threshold check and truncates skill descriptions to 57 chars + ellipsis ("..." = 3 chars) before any consumer can render them. It is imported by agent/prompt_builder.py (line 20) and invoked from _parse_skill_file() at line 1090, whose return value (the already-truncated `desc`) is later fed into the available_skills index block injected into the system prompt at line 1352 via `f"    - {name}: {desc}"`. The output length is therefore ≤60 chars regardless of the original frontmatter value.

targetSites.surroundingContext:
def _parse_skill_file(skill_file: Path) -> tuple[bool, dict, str]:
    """Parse a SKILL.md and return (is_compatible, frontmatter, description).

    ``description`` is the already-truncated form returned by
    ``extract_skill_description`` so callers can render the skill index
    without re-applying the cap.
    \"\"\"
    try:
        raw = skill_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")
        frontmatter, _ = parse_frontmatter(raw)

        if not skill_matches_platform(frontmatter):
            return False, frontmatter, \"\"

        # Environment relevance gate (offer-time only): hide skills tagged for
        # a runtime environment that isn't active (e.g. kanban-only skills for
        # non-kanban users, s6-only skills outside the container). Explicit
        # loads (skill_view / --skills) bypass this — see skill_matches_environment.
        if not skill_matches_environment(frontmatter):
            return False, frontmatter, \"\"

        return True, frontmatter, extract_skill_description(frontmatter)
targetSites.whyThisIsTheTruncation: DIRECT CALL SITE. This is the only call to extract_skill_description in production code. The truncated string is then flowed into _build_snapshot_entry → skills_by_category → the index_lines.append at line 1352 that becomes part of the <available_skills> block in build_skills_system_prompt(). The docstring even says: \"description is the already-truncated form returned by extract_skill_description so callers can render the skill index without re-applying the cap.\"

githubIssueSummary: Issue #46005 \"Skill description YAML bug (15 files) + 57-char truncation too restrictive for CJK\" — confirms the 57-char + \"...\" truncation (total rendered width 60) is the current Hermes behavior and is considered too restrictive for CJK / non-ASCII scripts. Open / P3. Sibling issue #46024 \"Make skill description truncation limit configurable\" is the feature-request counterpart (Open / P3).

otherRelatedSites.relevance: DISTINCT (unrelated) cap. Applies to skills_list() / skill_view() tool output for the full frontmatter — clamps at 1024 chars (not 60). This is the second-tier limit when the LLM calls skills_list as a tool; it is NOT the one that affects the injected system prompt index.</relevance>

Same 1024-char MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH cap from skills_list() / _find_all_skills() — only matters when the LLM invokes skills_list mid-conversation, NOT for the auto-injected system prompt index. Lines 810-811 repeat this for the plugin-skill view path.</relevance>
CLI __main__ self-test only — operator-facing print, never injected into agent context. Cosmetic 60-char preview.</relevance>
Hub catalog listing — operator TUI only. Not on the agent-injection path.</relevance>
